---
title: Terraform / OpenTofu Evidence Adapter — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: terraform
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-16'
---

# Terraform / OpenTofu Evidence Adapter — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Terraform / OpenTofu Evidence Adapter is the **IaC drift-detection edge** in the UIAO substrate. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it consumes Terraform and OpenTofu artifacts — state files (`.tfstate`), plan output (`terraform plan -json`), and HCL configuration files (`.tf`) — to produce object-keyed canonical claims with KSI-anchored provenance.

This is the **most comprehensive drift-detection adapter** in the family. Most adapters compare two views — declared vs observed. The Terraform adapter compares three: live system state (via a companion adapter), Terraform state, and HCL configuration. Any pair of those three can disagree, and the adapter reports each misalignment with the affected resource, attribute, and severity. That three-way view catches drift modes that two-way comparison misses: state file lying about what's deployed, config drifted from what the team thinks is applied, manual changes that bypass Terraform entirely.

Three primary functions:

1. **State extraction.** Parses Terraform v4 state files to enumerate all managed resources across providers (AWS, Azure, GCP, etc.), producing one `ClaimObject` per resource with full attribute preservation and deterministic provenance hashes.
2. **Plan consumption.** Ingests `terraform plan -json` output and maps each planned action (create, update, delete) to a severity-rated `DriftReport`, which feeds downstream POA&M generation through the OSCAL pipeline.
3. **Three-way drift detection.** Compares live system state, Terraform state, and HCL configuration; emits per-resource diff findings with the source-of-truth attribution (which of the three sources holds the canonical value).

The adapter never invokes the `terraform` CLI directly. It parses the output files that the CLI produces, maintaining a clean separation between infrastructure execution and governance observation.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/terraform_adapter.py` + `src/uiao/adapters/terraform_parser.py` (real parsing, not stubs). End-to-end OSCAL SAR generation proven via 7 integration tests. Subclasses `DatabaseAdapterBase`; five Terraform-specific extension methods (`extract_terraform_state`, `parse_hcl_config`, `consume_terraform_plan`, `detect_terraform_drift`, `generate_terraform_evidence`) sit on top of the seven canonical adapter domains.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `terraform-state`, `terraform-plan`, `hcl-configuration`

**Reads:** Terraform v4 state files (JSON) — local files, S3 backends, or Terraform Cloud state URLs; Terraform plan output (JSON from `terraform plan -json`) for planned infrastructure changes; HCL2 configuration files (`.tf`) parsed via `python-hcl2` for desired-state declarations.

**Emits:** `ClaimSet` with one `ClaimObject` per managed resource (from state or HCL), `DriftReport` with per-resource planned changes and severity ratings (from plan), `EvidenceObject` with bundled provenance chain, plus the `terraform-claim-manifest.json`, `terraform-drift-report.json`, and `terraform-ksi-bundle.json` artifacts. OSCAL Assessment Results (SAR) via the `adapter_to_oscal.py` bridge.

**Does NOT:** invoke `terraform apply` or make any infrastructure changes, access cloud provider APIs directly (that is the companion live-system adapter's job), modify `.tfstate` files (read-only), or store credentials (authentication is delegated to the runner environment).

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-2`, `CM-3`, `CM-6`, `CM-8`, `CA-7`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-2** Baseline Configuration | Primary | State extraction captures the current baseline of all managed infrastructure resources as the canonical record. |
| **CM-3** Configuration Change Control | Primary | Plan consumption maps every planned change to a drift item with create / update / delete action and field-level diff before any apply. |
| **CM-6** Configuration Settings | Supporting | HCL parsing captures declared configuration settings; three-way drift detects deviations from the declared state. |
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Primary | State extraction produces a complete, deterministic inventory of every Terraform-managed component with provider, type, name, and attribute data. |
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Supporting | When run on a schedule, the adapter provides continuous evidence of infrastructure drift between desired (HCL), recorded (state), and actual (live) configurations. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Consumes Terraform / OpenTofu state, plans, and HCL configuration to produce object-keyed canonical claims with KSI provenance, supports three-way drift detection (live ↔ state ↔ HCL), and enables controlled change-making actions against target environments (per UIAO_003 §4.7 Integration Adapter Class).

Adapter shipped in uiao as `TerraformAdapter(DatabaseAdapterBase)` (WhalerMike/uiao#8, commit 3635110). Status flipped from `reserved` to `active` per the three-stage lifecycle: canon (Stage 1, uiao#183), docs scaffolds (Stage 2, uiao-docs#26), impl code (Stage 3, uiao#8). The five Terraform-specific extension methods are currently stubs; real parsing ships as a follow-up when the `python-hcl2` dependency is added.

ZTMM pillars: applications-and-workloads, networks.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- https://www.terraform.io
- https://opentofu.org

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
